Purpose: Defines envelope meta-schema requiring type and schema fields for TCMS documents
Notes: Core meta-schema for versioned envelope format. Referenced by all v1 schemas. Defines document types: test_case, test_execution, test_verification, test_result, container_config, test_results_container

Purpose: Test case definition schema with envelope support
Notes: Complete test case schema with envelope. Includes prerequisites, hooks, BDD initial conditions, test sequences with steps, hydration_vars. Uses draft-07. References ../tcms-envelope.schema.json

Purpose: Test execution log entry schema with envelope support
Notes: Execution log entry for individual test steps. Includes test_sequence, step, command, exit_code, output, timestamp, hook_type, verification flags. Uses draft-07. References envelope

Purpose: Test case verification result schema with envelope support
Notes: Verification results with envelope. Includes test_case_id, sequences with step results (Pass/Fail/NotExecuted), total/passed/failed/not_executed counts, overall_pass. Uses draft-07. Has optional requirement/item/tc fields

Purpose: Test verification output schema with envelope support
Notes: Similar to test-verification.schema.v1.json. Test result schema with envelope. Includes test_case_id, sequences, step counts, overall_pass. Uses draft-07. Has optional requirement/item/tc fields

Purpose: Test case results container schema with envelope support
Notes: Container for multiple test results. Includes title, project, test_date, test_results array, metadata (environment, platform, executor, execution metrics). Uses draft-07. Full envelope support

Purpose: Container format configuration schema with envelope support
Notes: Configuration for wrapping test results with metadata. Includes title, project, optional environment/platform/executor. Uses draft-07. Full envelope support with examples

Purpose: Legacy test case verification result schema
Notes: Legacy verification schema without envelope. Uses draft-07. Same structure as v1 but lacks type/schema envelope fields. Has optional requirement/item/tc at top level only (not on sequences/steps)

Purpose: Legacy test verification output schema - simplified version
Notes: Legacy verification schema without envelope. Similar to verification_schema.json but with simplified structure. Uses draft-07. No requirement/item/tc fields at all. Simpler Fail structure (missing expected/actual fields)

Purpose: Legacy test results container schema - minimal version
Notes: Legacy container without envelope. Has title, project, test_date, test_results, metadata. Uses draft-07. Less detailed than v1 (test_results items are loosely typed as 'object', metadata requires environment/platform/executor but v1 requires execution_duration/counts)

Purpose: Legacy test results container schema - expanded version
Notes: Legacy container without envelope. More detailed than test_results/container_schema.json. Has complex definitions for TestStepExecution, expectedOnlyPair, passFailResult. Includes requirement/item/tc at test result level. Uses draft-07. Metadata same as test_results version

Purpose: Minimal legacy container schema
Duplication Status: LEGACY - Superseded by container-config and test-results-container schemas
Notes: Very minimal schema with only date, product, description fields. Uses draft-04. Appears to be an early/incomplete container format. Does not match current container concepts
Recommended Action: Deprecate - Too minimal to be useful

Purpose: Legacy test case schema with optional envelope support
Notes: Similar to v1 but uses draft-04. Has optional type/schema fields for envelope support but not required. Same comprehensive structure with prerequisites, hooks, BDD initial conditions, test sequences. Nearly identical to v1 but older JSON schema version

Purpose: Legacy container format configuration with optional envelope support
Notes: Similar to v1 but uses draft-07. Has optional type/schema fields for envelope support. Same structure with title, project, environment, platform, executor. Includes examples. Main difference: envelope fields optional vs required

Purpose: Legacy test execution log entry with optional envelope support
Notes: Similar to v1 but uses draft-07. Has optional type/schema fields for envelope support. Same structure with test_sequence, step, command, exit_code, output, timestamp, hook_type, verification flags. Additional note about manual steps in output description

Purpose: Legacy test verification output schema with optional envelope support
Notes: Similar to test-result.schema.v1.json but with optional envelope fields. Uses draft-07. Has test_case_id, sequences, step results (Pass/Fail/NotExecuted), counts, overall_pass. Optional requirement/item/tc fields. Externally tagged enum for step results

Purpose: Legacy test case verification result with optional envelope support
Notes: Similar to test-verification.schema.v1.json but with optional envelope fields. Uses draft-07. Has test_case_id, sequences with step results, counts, overall_pass. Optional requirement/item/tc at multiple levels. Externally tagged enum for step results

Purpose: Verification method schema for test-based verification
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for test verification method type. Uses draft-04. Includes requirement, item, tc, test_sequences with steps. Simpler than main test-case schema. Part of verification methods framework
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system

Purpose: Verification method schema for analysis-based verification
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for analysis verification method. Uses draft-04. Includes calculations, models, acceptance_criteria. For analytical verification approaches
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system

Purpose: Verification method schema for demonstration-based verification
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for demonstration verification method. Uses draft-04. Includes procedure, observations, acceptance_criteria. For operational demonstrations
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system

Purpose: Verification method schema for inspection-based verification
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for inspection verification method. Uses draft-04. Includes inspection_method, checklist, acceptance_criteria. For inspection/review verification
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system

Purpose: Verification method schema for Common Criteria security evaluation
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for Common Criteria evaluation. Uses draft-04. Comprehensive security evaluation schema with EAL levels, protection profiles, SFRs, SARs, TOE security functions, test coverage, vulnerability assessment, evidence documents. For high-security systems
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system

Purpose: Verification method schema for high-assurance (DO-178C) verification
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for DO-178C high-assurance verification. Uses draft-04. Includes DAL levels (A-E), verification objectives, traceability, structural coverage (statement/decision/MC/DC), verification activities, configuration management. For safety-critical aviation software
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system

Purpose: Verification method schema for result reporting
Duplication Status: Unique - Part of verification methods collection
Notes: Schema for reporting verification results. Uses draft-04. Includes test_case_id, sequences, step_results, counts, overall_pass. Generic result format for verification methods
Recommended Action: Keep - Part of verification methods system
